original: Holographic mirror systems use an array of phase-shifted holographic projectors to produce a small squadron of life-like images of the user’s own ship in order to distract and confuse hostiles. The holograms produce convincing spoofed sensor data, but cannot be maintained through any amount of damage. One hologram can be maintained per 25 points of systems to a maximum of 3. Crowded battlefields may reduce the number of holograms produced. (1x per fight)
context: <<POS:07546727>> aphic mirror system",e.tooltip='Holographic mirror systems use an array of phase-shifted hol'

original: The battlefield is too crowded for any holograms to be created!
context: <<POS:07547941>> combatOutput("The battlefield is too crowded for any holograms to be created!",null,t)

original: You tap the toggle to deploy your holographic duplicates, and produce "+{var0}+".
context: <<POS:07548303>> <<VARS:var0=["ERROR","one photonic dummy","two photonic dummies","three photonic dummies"][n]>> combatOutput("You tap the toggle to deploy your holographic duplicates, and produce "+["ERROR","one photonic dummy","two photonic dummies","three photonic dummies"][n]+".",null,t)

original: {var1}.capitalize({var0})+" is abruptly part of a squadron! "+{var2}+" materialize around it. Which one is real?!
context: <<POS:07548482>> <<VARS:var0=t.getCombatName(),!1,var1=h.m,var2=["ERROR","One photonic dummy","Two photonic dummies","Three photonic dummies"][n]>> combatOutput(h.m.capitalize(t.getCombatName(),!1)+" is abruptly part of a squadron!
